content: httpx AsyncClient lifecycle in FastAPI: Creating a new AsyncClient per request (async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client) is correct for background tasks and route handlers. Reusing a module-level AsyncClient is a pattern that works BUT requires app lifespan management — the client must be created in lifespan startup and closed in lifespan shutdown; closing it in a module finalizer is unreliable. For GitHubIntegration: the safest pattern is create-per-call (async with httpx.AsyncClient()) inside each method, with shared base_url/headers/timeout in __init__. For high-throughput production, an app-lifespan-managed client is preferred, but for PoC the per-call pattern avoids event loop and cleanup pitfalls.
